sections.title: -AR Verbs
sections.explanation: Most Spanish verbs belong to the -AR group. To conjugate them in the present tense, remove the -ar ending and add the personal endings: yo -o, tú -as, él/ella -a, nosotros -amos, vosotros -áis, ellos/ellas -an. Example: hablar (to speak) → stem: habl- → yo hablo, tú hablas, él/ella habla, nosotros hablamos, vosotros habláis, ellos/ellas hablan. Other common -AR verbs: caminar (to walk), trabajar (to work), escuchar (to listen), cocinar (to cook). The pattern is identical for all regular -AR verbs.
sections.tip: The yo form always ends in -o for regular -AR verbs. The tú form ends in -as, and the ellos/ellas form ends in -an. Notice that vosotros -áis carries a written accent.

exercises.prompt: What is the correct form of 'hablar' (to speak) for 'yo'?
exercises.explanation: The yo form of regular -AR verbs drops -ar and adds -o: habl + o = hablo.

exercises.prompt: Tú ___ (caminar) al trabajo. (You walk to work.)
exercises.hint: The tú ending for -AR verbs is -as
exercises.explanation: caminar → stem caMin- + as = caminas. Tú caminas al trabajo.

exercises.prompt: How do you conjugate 'escuchar' (to listen) for 'ella'?
exercises.explanation: The él/ella form of -AR verbs takes the -a ending: escuch + a = escucha.

exercises.prompt: Nosotros ___ (cocinar) la cena. (We cook dinner.)
exercises.hint: The nosotros ending for -AR verbs is -amos
exercises.explanation: cocinar → stem cocin- + amos = cocinamos. Nosotros cocinamos la cena.

exercises.prompt: What is the correct form of 'trabajar' (to work) for 'ellos'?
exercises.explanation: The ellos/ellas form of -AR verbs takes -an: trabaj + an = trabajan.

exercises.prompt: Match each pronoun to the correct -AR ending:
exercises.explanation: Regular -AR verb endings: yo -o, tú -as, él/ella -a, nosotros -amos, vosotros -áis, ellos/ellas -an.

exercises.prompt: Vosotros ___ (hablar) muy rápido. (You all speak very fast.)
exercises.hint: The vosotros ending for -AR verbs is -áis (with an accent)
exercises.explanation: hablar → habl + áis = habláis. The vosotros form always carries a written accent on the á.

exercises.prompt: Which sentence is correct?
exercises.explanation: Third person singular (él/ella) takes -a: camina. The other options use the wrong endings.

sections.title: -ER and -IR Verbs
sections.explanation: The second and third groups of Spanish verbs end in -er and -ir. They share most endings, but differ in the nosotros and vosotros forms. For -ER verbs (comer, beber), remove -er and add: yo -o, tú -es, él/ella -e, nosotros -emos, vosotros -éis, ellos/ellas -en. For -IR verbs (vivir, escribir), remove -ir and add: yo -o, tú -es, él/ella -e, nosotros -imos, vosotros -ís, ellos/ellas -en. The key difference: nosotros uses -emos for -ER verbs but -imos for -IR verbs; vosotros uses -éis for -ER verbs but -ís for -IR verbs.
sections.tip: The yo form is always -o for both groups. -ER and -IR verbs are identical in the yo, tú, él/ella, and ellos/ellas forms. Only nosotros and vosotros differ: -emos/-éis for -ER, -imos/-ís for -IR.

exercises.prompt: What is the correct form of 'comer' (to eat) for 'yo'?
exercises.explanation: The yo form of -ER verbs drops -er and adds -o: com + o = como.

exercises.prompt: Tú ___ (beber) café por las mañanas. (You drink coffee in the mornings.)
exercises.hint: The tú ending for -ER verbs is -es
exercises.explanation: beber → stem beb- + es = bebes. Tú bebes café por las mañanas.

exercises.prompt: How do you conjugate 'vivir' (to live) for 'nosotros'?
exercises.explanation: -IR verbs use -imos for nosotros: viv + imos = vivimos. This is different from -ER verbs which use -emos (comemos).

exercises.prompt: What is the vosotros form of 'comer' (to eat)?
exercises.explanation: -ER verbs use -éis for vosotros: com + éis = coméis. Compare with -IR verbs which use -ís (vivís).

exercises.prompt: Ellos ___ (escribir) un libro. (They write a book.)
exercises.hint: The ellos ending for both -ER and -IR verbs is -en
exercises.explanation: escribir → stem escrib- + en = escriben. Both -ER and -IR verbs use -en for ellos/ellas.

exercises.prompt: Match each nosotros/vosotros form to the correct verb group:
exercises.explanation: The nosotros and vosotros forms are what distinguish -ER from -IR verbs: -emos/-éis vs -imos/-ís.

exercises.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'beber' for 'él'?
exercises.explanation: Third person singular of -ER verbs takes -e: beb + e = bebe. Él bebe vino.

exercises.prompt: Ella ___ (vivir) cerca de aquí. (She lives near here.)
exercises.hint: The él/ella ending for -IR verbs is -e
exercises.explanation: vivir → stem viv- + e = vive. The él/ella form is the same for both -ER and -IR verbs.

sections.title: Irregular Present Tense Verbs
sections.explanation: Many of the most common Spanish verbs are irregular in the present tense. Some are irregular only in the yo form; others are irregular throughout. Key irregular verbs: ser (to be [permanent]): soy, eres, es, somos, sois, son. estar (to be [temporary/location]): estoy, estás, está, estamos, estáis, están. ir (to go): voy, vas, va, vamos, vais, van. tener (to have): tengo, tienes, tiene, tenemos, tenéis, tienen. hacer (to do/make): hago, haces, hace, hacemos, hacéis, hacen. poder (to be able to/can): puedo, puedes, puede, podemos, podéis, pueden. querer (to want/love): quiero, quieres, quiere, queremos, queréis, quieren. Notice that tener, hacer, and ir have unique yo forms (tengo, hago, voy) that do not follow regular patterns.
sections.tip: Focus on memorizing the yo forms first — soy, estoy, voy, tengo, hago, puedo, quiero. Once you know the yo form, the other forms often follow a more regular pattern (though ser and ir are irregular throughout).

examples.en: He has a lot of work.

exercises.prompt: What is the yo form of 'ser' (to be)?
exercises.explanation: ser is fully irregular: yo soy, tú eres, él/ella es, nosotros somos, vosotros sois, ellos/ellas son.

exercises.prompt: Yo ___ (estar) muy bien, gracias. (I am very well, thank you.)
exercises.hint: The yo form of 'estar' is irregular
exercises.explanation: estar: yo estoy. The -oy ending is shared by both estar and ir in the yo form (estoy, voy).

exercises.prompt: How do you say 'We go to school' in Spanish?
exercises.explanation: ir for nosotros is vamos: Nosotros vamos a la escuela. Note that vamos is also used for 'Let's go!'

exercises.prompt: What is the correct yo form of 'tener' (to have)?
exercises.explanation: tener has an irregular yo form: tengo. The other forms are mostly regular with the stem tien- (tienes, tiene) or ten- (tenemos, tenéis, tienen).

exercises.prompt: Match each irregular verb to its yo form:
exercises.explanation: Irregular yo forms: soy (ser), estoy (estar), voy (ir), tengo (tener), hago (hacer), puedo (poder), quiero (querer).

exercises.prompt: Él ___ (poder) hablar tres idiomas. (He can speak three languages.)
exercises.hint: poder is a stem-changing verb: o→ue in most forms
exercises.explanation: poder: puedo, puedes, puede, podemos, podéis, pueden. The o→ue change occurs in all forms except nosotros and vosotros.

exercises.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'querer' (to want)?
exercises.explanation: querer: yo quiero. The o→ie stem change gives quiero, quieres, quiere — but queremos and queréis keep the original stem.

exercises.prompt: What is the difference between 'ser' and 'estar'?
options: 'ser' is for permanent traits; 'estar' is for temporary states and location
exercises.explanation: ser expresses identity, origin, and permanent characteristics (soy estudiante, es español). estar expresses temporary states, emotions, and location (estoy cansado, está en casa).
